outbound_official.note: no official Uganda government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Uganda's Directorate of Citizenship and Immigration Control (immigration.go.ug) and Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mofa.go.ug) publish only inbound visa policy pages (which nationalities may enter Uganda without a visa). The March 2026 announcement about '40 visa-free countries' was Uganda granting inbound visa-free entry to citizens of those countries, not a list of destinations where Uganda ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. No origin-side official page equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin was found for Uganda.

name: Uganda

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Single Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism, leisure, and visiting friends or family in Uganda. Must be applied for online before travel.
visa_types.notes: Mandatory pre-travel e-visa via visas.immigration.go.ug. Applicant receives an email authorization letter with barcode; border officials complete visa issuance on arrival. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond arrival date.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (6–12 months)
visa_types.purpose: Allows multiple entries into Uganda for tourism and short visits over a 6 to 12-month validity period.
visa_types.notes: Applied online through visas.immigration.go.ug. Valid for 6–12 months from approval date. USD 100 fee. Each stay duration within Uganda is governed by conditions set at entry.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (24 months)
visa_types.purpose: Allows multiple entries into Uganda for tourism and short visits over a 24-month validity period.
visa_types.notes: Applied online through visas.immigration.go.ug. Valid for 24 months from approval date. USD 200 fee.

visa_types.name: East African Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Regional multi-country tourist visa allowing multiple entries into Uganda, Kenya, and Rwanda for tourism purposes.
visa_types.notes: Joint visa issued by Uganda, Kenya, and Rwanda. Valid for 90 days with multiple entries across all three countries. Apply online. Confirmed by UK GOV travel advice at USD 100.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For travelers passing through Uganda to a third country who require a visa and will remain in Uganda for a short transit period.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via visas.immigration.go.ug before travel. Fee USD 50. Exact max transit stay not specified on official pages reviewed.

visa_types.name: Business Visa (Single Entry)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting Uganda for business meetings, conferences, trade negotiations, or commercial activities that do not constitute employment.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via Uganda's Electronic Visa and Permit Application System. Business visitors engaging in any form of employment or paid work must obtain a work permit instead. Canada travel advisory confirms business visa as a distinct category.

visa_types.name: Entry Permit – Class G (Employment / Work Permit)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals employed by a Ugandan company or organization. Required for any form of paid employment, including missionary and volunteer work.
visa_types.notes: Class G is the standard employment entry permit. Subclass G3 covers rare-skilled expatriates in agro-processing, manufacturing, and mining. EAC nationals (Kenya, Rwanda) are exempt from work permit fees; Tanzanian nationals pay reduced fee of USD 1,550/year. Applied online via visas.immigration.go.ug. US State Dept advisory warns that missionary and volunteer work frequently require a work visa; operating without valid permit may result in fines or imprisonment.

visa_types.name: Entry Permit – Investor Classes (B, C, D, E, F)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who are shareholders or investors in Ugandan companies across sectors including agriculture, agro-processing, mining, manufacturing, general trade, and specific professions.
visa_types.notes: Class B1: Agriculture shareholders. Class B2: Agro-processing (fees: 6 months USD 400; 12 months USD 400; 24 months available). Class C1/C2: Prospecting/mining of minerals. Class D: General trade. Class E: Manufacturing. Class F: Specific professions. No minimum investment threshold stated on official pages. Linked to active investment in Uganda companies.

visa_types.name: Entry Permit – Class H (Independent Means / Ordinary Resident)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have assured income derived from sources outside Uganda and commit not to accept any employment in Uganda.
visa_types.notes: Closest equivalent to a retirement/passive income residence permit. No minimum income threshold stated on official pages. Holder must not engage in any form of employment within Uganda.

visa_types.name: Student Pass
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled as full-time students at a recognized educational institution in Uganda.
visa_types.notes: Student Pass is referenced as a valid Uganda residence document on official immigration pages. Holders of a valid Student Pass do not require a visa to re-enter Uganda. Specific fee and validity terms not published on accessible official pages.

visa_types.name: Dependant's Pass
visa_types.purpose: For dependants (spouse, children) of foreign nationals holding a valid Entry Permit, Student Pass, or other Uganda residence authorization.
visa_types.notes: Dependant's Pass is listed as a valid Uganda residence document on official immigration pages. Holders do not require a visa to re-enter Uganda while their pass is valid. Specific fee and validity details not published on accessible official pages.

visa_types.name: Special Pass
visa_types.purpose: A short-term authorization issued to foreign nationals awaiting processing of an Entry Permit or for other special circumstances requiring temporary residence status.
visa_types.notes: Special Pass is listed as a valid Uganda residence document on official immigration pages. Holders do not require a visa to re-enter Uganda while their pass is valid. Commonly issued as a bridge document while an Entry Permit application is being processed.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Official Passport Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry to Uganda for holders of diplomatic or official passports of certain countries under bilateral agreements.
visa_types.notes: Countries whose diplomatic and official passport holders are exempt: Algeria, Cuba, Egypt, Ethiopia, India, Iran, Russia, Senegal, South Africa, Venezuela, Vietnam. Turkey: diplomatic passport only (not official). These exemptions are based on bilateral visa abolition agreements. Ordinary passport holders from these countries still require an e-visa.

visa_free.notes: Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Nationals with Conventional Travel Documents (CTDs) issued by any country are equally exempt. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.

Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Kenyan nationals are also exempted from work permit fees under EAC Common Market Protocol. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.

Visa waiver agreement implemented in Uganda as of February 28, 2025 (Fragomen alert). Nationals may stay 30–90 days for business or tourism, with exact duration at the discretion of immigration authorities. Confirmed in the March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs 40-country visa-free list.

Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Rwandan nationals are also exempted from work permit fees under EAC Common Market Protocol. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.

Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement. Note: Wikipedia states South Sudan nationals receive 6 months visa-free - this claim could not be verified against a live official source and is recorded in gaps.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Listed as 'Swaziland' on official page. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.

Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Work permit fees revised to USD 1,550 per year for Tanzanian nationals under EAC protocol. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.

Bilateral visa waiver agreement signed May 7, 2025, effective February 10, 2026. UAE nationals holding diplomatic, special, ordinary, and service passports are all exempt from visa requirements for entry into Uganda for stays up to 90 days. Agreement confirmed by Uganda Broadcasting Corporation (ubc.go.ug) and multiple official sources. Ordinary Ugandan passport holders traveling to the UAE still require a visa.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.
Visa abolition agreement. No visa required. Listed as 'Hong Kong SAR' on official page. Max stay 90 days per March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement.

e_visa.notes: All visa-required travelers must apply online at visas.immigration.go.ug BEFORE travel. No visas are issued on arrival. Single-entry tourist visa: USD 50, valid up to 3 months (90 days) from approval date. Transit visa: USD 50. Multiple-entry visas: 6–12 months USD 100; 24 months USD 200. East African Tourist Visa (Uganda, Kenya, Rwanda joint): USD 100, 90 days for tourism. Processing time: 2–3 days. Uganda operates a mandatory pre-travel e-visa system.

visa_policy.notes: Uganda operates a mandatory pre-travel e-visa/online visa system - no visas are issued on arrival. Visa-exempt nationals present their documents at the port of entry. The official visa-exempt list on immigration.go.ug is authoritative (40 countries/territories). The March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement confirmed all 40 visa-free nationalities may stay up to 90 days for tourism or business. The Uganda-UAE bilateral agreement (signed May 7, 2025, effective February 10, 2026) covers UAE nationals with ordinary, diplomatic, special, and service passports for up to 90 days. The Uganda-Mozambique visa waiver was implemented February 28, 2025 (30–90 days at immigration discretion). A 2021 notice also listed Angola, Cuba, and Libya as visa-fee exempt; however, those nationalities do not appear on the current dedicated visa-exempt-countries page on immigration.go.ug and are not included as visa-free here. The East African Tourist Visa (USD 100) is a joint visa valid for Uganda, Kenya, and Rwanda for 90 days for tourism purposes. Multiple-entry visa tiers confirmed as: 6–12 months USD 100; 24 months USD 200.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Uganda government source. Uganda does not offer a CBI or golden passport program. Uganda does allow dual citizenship: for non-Ugandans the application fee is USD 200 non-refundable + USD 5,000 upon approval. Citizenship by naturalisation requires 20 years of residence; citizenship by registration requires marriage to a Ugandan national. Source: https://www.immigration.go.ug/citizenship/dual and https://www.immigration.go.ug/citizenship/naturalisation

programs.name: Entry Permit – Investor Classes (B, C, D, E)
programs.notes: Uganda offers several investor-oriented Entry Permit classes for foreign shareholders. Class B1: shareholders of companies in Agriculture. Class B2: Agro-processing (6 months: USD 400; 12 months: USD 400; 24 months available). Class C1/C2: prospecting/mining of minerals. Class D: general trade. Class E: manufacturing. Class F: specific professions. These are residence-linked permits tied to active investment in Uganda companies. No explicit minimum investment amount is stated on the official page. No formal golden visa or RBI pathway to permanent residence or citizenship is documented on official sources.

programs.name: Entry Permit – Class H (Ordinary Resident / Independent Means)
programs.notes: Class H is issued to persons who have an assured income derived from sources outside Uganda and undertake not to accept employment of any kind in Uganda. No minimum income amount stated on official page.

programs.name: Entry Permit – Class G3 (Rare Skilled Expatriates)
programs.eligibility: Expatriates with rare skills employed in agro-processing, manufacturing, and mining of selected minerals
programs.notes: Class G3 is a special category for rare-skilled foreign nationals. No fast-track fee waiver or accelerated timeline stated on official page beyond standard work permit processing.

programs.name: East African Community Work Permit Fee Waiver (Kenya & Rwanda nationals)
programs.eligibility: Nationals of Kenya and Rwanda under EAC Common Market Protocol
programs.notes: Nationals from Kenya and Rwanda are exempted from payment of immigration fees; however, they must still apply and obtain a work permit. Tanzanian nationals pay reduced fee of USD 1,550 per year.

agreements: East African Community (EAC) Common Market Protocol – free movement of persons, labour, services and capital among EAC Partner States

credential.subtype: Certificate of Residence, Entry/Work Permit, Student Pass, Dependant's Pass, or Special Pass (valid Uganda-issued residence document)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid Ugandan residence (Certificate of Residence, Entry/Work Permit, Student Pass, Dependant's Pass, Special Pass) do not require a visa to return to Uganda if they show proof of a valid residence in Uganda. This applies to any nationality that would otherwise require a visa.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Algeria do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Cuba do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Egypt do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Ethiopia do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of India do not require a visa to travel to Uganda. Ordinary Indian passport holders require an e-visa.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Iran do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Russia do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Senegal do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of South Africa do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic passports of Turkey only (not official passports) do not require a visa to travel to Uganda. The official page specifies 'Only Diplomatic' for Turkey.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Venezuela do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.
Holders of diplomatic and official passports of Vietnam do not require a visa to travel to Uganda under the bilateral visa abolition agreement.

credential.subtype: Laissez-Passer (UN, AU, ADB, COMESA, IMF/World Bank, IGAD, IATA, ELCI, ILO, PTA/COMESA Bank, EU, CIP, ARC, AHF)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Laissez-Passers issued by international organizations recognized by Uganda or to which Uganda is a member are exempt from visa requirements whilst on official business. Organizations: United Nations, African Union, African Development Bank, COMESA, IMF/World Bank, IGAD, IATA, ELCI, ILO, PTA/COMESA Bank, European Union, International Potato Centre (CIP), African Reinsurance Corporation (ARC), African Housing Fund (AHF).

credential.subtype: Serving Member of the British Military (active duty status)

conditional_access.conditions: Serving members of the British Military are exempted from visa formalities when travelling to Uganda.

data_quality.validation_notes: Re-validated 2026-06-29 against live official and official-adjacent sources. immigration.go.ug returned HTTP 403 on all direct page fetches during this session (same as previous run 2026-06-27); bujumbura.mofa.go.ug also returned 403; ubc.go.ug returned HTTP 525 (SSL error). No changes were made to the visa_policy or conditional_access lists — all entries confirmed as follows: (1) The full 40-country visa-free list (39 nationalities + Hong Kong SAR) was re-confirmed by cross-referencing the March 2026 Ministry of Internal Affairs announcement via Pulse Uganda (verbatim official list) and Africanews article (which independently lists the same 39 nationalities). DRC (Democratic Republic of Congo) is confirmed on the official immigration.go.ug visa-exempt list per Google index snippet and the Pulse Uganda verbatim announcement — a separate EAC fee-waiver announcement is distinct and does not undermine the visa-free status. (2) UAE ordinary passport holder visa-free status re-confirmed: the bilateral agreement signed May 7, 2025 (effective February 10, 2026) explicitly covers UAE nationals with 'diplomatic, special, ordinary, and service passports' for up to 90 days in Uganda. The UBC article headline ('Diplomatic and Service Passport Holders') is narrower than the actual agreement text — the agreement covers ordinary passports as well, confirmed by Pulse Uganda inside-deal article and travelandtourworld.com. UAE entry in visa_free is correctly placed. (3) Max stay 90 days for all visa-free nationals remains confirmed per the March 2026 announcement. No new visa-free countries have been added since the March 2026 announcement. PREVIOUS CORRECTIONS (2026-06-27): max_stay_days updated from null to 90 for ALL visa-free entries; UAE source_url updated to UBC article; Mozambique notes updated to reflect Feb 28, 2025 implementation date. No further corrections needed in this run.
gaps: Exact max_stay_days for visa-free nationals was confirmed as 90 days in the March 2026 Ministry announcement; however, the official immigration.go.ug page was inaccessible (HTTP 403) for direct verification of whether the page itself now states '90 days'.

South Sudan: Wikipedia states South Sudan nationals receive 6 months visa-free (not 90 days like other exempt nations). Could not confirm this exception against a live official source. Record shows 90 days (matching general announcement); 6-month exception unverified.

Angola, Cuba (ordinary), and Libya appeared on the 2021 mandatory-notice list as visa-free but do not appear on the current visa-exempt-countries page or the March 2026 40-country list - their current ordinary-passport status remains unresolved. Excluded from visa_free per safety rule.

Italy and Namibia: Wikipedia states holders of diplomatic or service passports of Italy and Namibia enjoy visa-free stays. Italy and Namibia are not in the current conditional_access list. Could not confirm these two additions from a live official Uganda government source (immigration.go.ug returned 403). Not added pending official confirmation.
Minimum investment thresholds for investor entry permit classes (B, C, D, E) not stated on official pages - only fee structures for the permit itself.
No official digital-nomad or startup visa scheme found on official Uganda immigration pages.
No official CBI or formal RBI golden visa program confirmed; no minimum investment amounts for residency documented on official sources.
Formal pathway from Entry Permit to permanent residence or citizenship (years required) not stated on official immigration pages reviewed.

No official Uganda rule found granting visa-free/VoA/e-visa entry based on holding a valid third-country visa (e.g. US, UK, Schengen, Canada, Australia, Japan, UAE, GCC) or third-country residence permit/PR card. Exemptions are based on nationality only.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder conditional entry rule for Uganda found on any official source.
Exact dates of bilateral visa abolition agreements for the 12 diplomatic/official passport holder countries are not published on the official immigration.go.ug page.
Multiple-entry visa 36-month tier mentioned in 2021 notice is absent from the current dedicated multiple-entry visa page; could not confirm whether this tier was removed or simply not updated.
Business visa fee not explicitly confirmed as separate from tourist visa fee on official pages - listed as USD 50 based on the general e-visa fee structure.
Medical visa: no dedicated medical visa category found on official Uganda immigration pages.
Working holiday visa: no working holiday visa program found for Uganda.
Retirement visa: no dedicated retirement visa found - Class H (Independent Means) entry permit is the closest equivalent.

UAE diplomatic/service passports: the UAE bilateral agreement covers 'diplomatic, special, ordinary and service passports' of UAE nationals. Since ordinary passports are already in visa_free, the diplomatic/service dimension is noted here for completeness but no separate conditional_access entry was added (would be redundant).

UBC article (ubc.go.ug) cited as source for UAE deal now returns HTTP 525 (SSL error) as of 2026-06-29 - deal details confirmed via Pulse Uganda inside-deal article and travelandtourworld.com instead.
